markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Build fact graph projections from stable facts and accepted relationship edges. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: FactGraphProjection leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: FactGraphProjection is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified d39c2d76; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-117 requires a Handshake-native FactGraphProjection built from stable facts and accepted relationship edges, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and projection-never-authority semantics. The implementation in knowledge_memory/projection.rs delivers a real, SQL-backed projection with trusted-only gating (retrieval-trusted label + accepted claim + no unresolved conflict) and is proven against real managed PostgreSQL.
findings: build_fact_graph (src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_memory/projection.rs:160-198) reads real authority rows via list_memory_facts (PG), get_knowledge_claim (PG), get_knowledge_entity (PG); marks authority_class="projection" (line 196) per spec 2.3.13.11 projection-never-authority rule.

trusted_only filtering (projection.rs:174-187) correctly gates: retrieval-trusted label (claim_labels.rs:22 -> Source/Derived/OperatorApproved only), claim lifecycle == Accepted, and claim_has_unresolved_conflict via real SQL against knowledge_claim_conflicts (projection.rs:237-252). Deprecated/superseded/unsupported/model-suggested/proposed/conflicted facts excluded as the MT scope requires.

Test fact_graph_projection_trusted_only_filter (knowledge_memory_projection_tests.rs:128-265) runs against REAL Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (MemoryFixture::setup, skips if no PG) — proves all-facts (4 edges) vs trusted-only (1 edge), conflicted SOURCE facts excluded, accepted gating.

Two additional real-PG negative tests harden the gate: fact_graph_trusted_only_blocks_raw_sql_unresolved_conflicts (lines 267-364) inserts conflicts via raw SQL connection and proves accepted claims fall back to Conflicted; unsupported_fact_cannot_be_relabeled_into_trusted_stable_graph (lines 366-456) proves both API (StorageError::Conflict) and DB-trigger (migration 0321) reject relabeling unsupported -> trusted.

Migrations 0240-0243 (ontology/facts/conflict_jobs/bridge_edges) and 0321 (unsupported_fact_guard) exist with .down.sql rollbacks. Facts back knowledge_claims, reusing existing claim lifecycle + conflict authority rather than inventing a parallel authority store.

Sub-rule 3 NA: lifecycle.claimed_by and completed_by are null, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION — no self-certification. Implementation commit d39c2d76 touches only the test file; no /.GOV/, SQLite, Docker, or external-search paths in the diff.
Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): packet cites v02.192 anchors; resolved against current v02.194 (section 2.3.13.11 present and consistent). Not a failing reason.
evidence_checked: git show --stat d39c2d76 (impl commit, test-only diff)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
